qualifiers: 這是直接開啟同一原生 DuckDB file 的 boundary，不含 remote protocol 或外部 catalog。
claims.text: 直接開啟原生 DuckDB file 時，read-write mode 由單一 process 擁有；multiple processes 只適用於全員 read-only。owner process 內可有多個 writer threads，append 不衝突，同 row update/delete 則以 conflict error 失敗。
claims.would_change_if: DuckDB current documentation 明確放寬直接 file access 的 process boundary。

qualifiers: Quack 是 client-server boundary，不是多個 process 直接任意開啟同一 database file。
claims.text: 現行官方文件修正了「DuckDB 沒有任何 multi-process write 路徑」的絕對說法：Quack 可把 DuckDB 轉成 client-server 以支援 multi-process writes，但目前仍是 beta，protocol、functions、settings 與 defaults 都可能改變。
claims.would_change_if: Quack 成為 stable，且 Parallax 的 failure-path probe 證明其操作成本低於 single-owner boundary。

qualifiers: 本次只確認一條 production path；不推論所有 connection lifecycle 都已集中治理。
claims.text: Parallax 目前 pin DuckDB 1.5.x，且 production code 已明確先關閉 read-only connection，才呼叫會開 read-write connection 的 repository，因同檔案 mixed read_only configuration 會被拒絕。
claims.would_change_if: Parallax 升級到不同 concurrency contract，或所有 consumers 改走 remote／snapshot boundary。

contract.question: 對 Parallax 的 single-writer local analytics workload，DuckDB 的 process/concurrency boundary 應如何制定？哪些官方限制與 failure modes 必須進入 acceptance tests？

acceptance_tests: owner process 以 read-write 開啟 temp DB，再由 subprocess 嘗試 read-only 直開同檔 => subprocess 在 timeout 內以預期 lock/access error 結束，owner transaction 與 DB integrity 不受損
owner 關閉後啟動兩個 read-only subprocess 查詢固定 snapshot => 兩者結果一致且任何 write statement 都失敗
同 process 兩個 connection/thread 並行 append 同 table => 所有 rows 存在且無 conflict
同 process 兩個 transactions 修改同一 row => 一方成功、另一方回報 transaction conflict；bounded retry 後結果唯一且可解釋
owner 在未 commit transaction 中被終止後重新開檔 => 未出現 partial write、沒有永久 lock，recovery 訊息可由 operator 判讀
若未來評估 Quack，先在 isolated temp DB 跑相同 failure suite => 未全綠前不得取代 single-owner production boundary
constraints: 本次研究沒有替 Parallax 實作上述 concurrency tests，也沒有對 Quack beta 做 production benchmark。
DuckDB current docs 會隨 v1.5.x／v2.0 演進；升版時必須重跑文件複驗與 failure-path suite。
本次 repository search 未找到明確的跨 process DuckDB lock falsifier。

safe_actions.description: 先新增一個 temp DB + subprocess 的 process-boundary test module，不改 production connection architecture。
把 Quack 列為 DuckDB stable 後的 revisit option；目前不安裝、不啟用。

evidence.excerpt:
In in-process mode, DuckDB has two configurable options for concurrency:

1. **Read-write mode:** one process can both read and write to the database.
2. **Read-only mode:** multiple processes can read from the database, but no processes can write ([`access_mode = 'READ_ONLY'`]({% link docs/current/configuration/overview.md %}#configuration-reference)).

When using read-write mode, DuckDB supports multiple writer threads using a combination of [MVCC (Multi-Version Concurrency Control)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multiversion_concurrency_control) and optimistic concurrency control (see [Concurrency within a Single Process](#concurrency-model-within-a-single-process)), but all within that single writer process. The reason for this concurrency model is to allow for the caching of data in RAM for faster analytical queries, rather than going back and forth to disk during each query. It also allows the caching of function pointers, the database catalog, and other items so that subsequent queries on the same connection are faster.

#### Concurrency Model within a Single Process

DuckDB supports concurrency within a single process according to the following rules. As long as there are no write conflicts, multiple concurrent writes will succeed. Appends will never conflict, even on the same table. Multiple threads can also simultaneously update separate tables or separate subsets of the same table. Optimistic concurrency control comes into play when two threads attempt to edit (update or delete) the same row at the same time. In that situation, the second thread to attempt the edit will fail with a conflict error.

### Multiple Processes

Writing to DuckDB's native database format from multiple processes is supported through the [Quack remote protocol]({% link docs/current/quack/overview.md %}), which turns DuckDB into a client-server database. Quack in beta stage as of DuckDB v1.5.2, and is expected to become mature by [DuckDB v2.0 in fall 2026]({% link release_calendar.md %}).

For a stable solution, consider using the [DuckLake](https://ducklake.select/) format with [PostgreSQL as the catalog database](https://ducklake.select/docs/stable/duckdb/usage/choosing_a_catalog_database#postgresql). By coordinating through a central PostgreSQL catalog, DuckDB instances can achieve concurrent read-writes on the same database. The DuckLake v1.0 specification and its DuckDB implementation, both intended for production use, were [published](https://ducklake.select/2026/04/13/ducklake-10/) in April 2026.

The Quack extension turns a DuckDB instance into a server that other DuckDB instances (clients) can connect to over HTTP.

This page covers the protocol at a glance and walks through basic usage on both sides of the wire.
For the full list of functions, settings, and logging knobs, see the [Reference]({% link docs/current/quack/reference.md %}). For configuring TLS and authentication / authorization, see [Security]({% link docs/current/quack/security.md %}). For user guides, see [Guides]({% link docs/current/quack/setup/overview.md %}).

> Warning Quack is under active development and the protocol, function names, settings, and defaults are still subject to change. This page documents the beta release of Quack, available in [DuckDB v1.5.3]({% post_url 2026-05-20-announcing-duckdb-153 %}), shipped via the `core` repository.

Opens a read-only DuckDB connection for the direct queries in this module
    (quantiles, history, hedge churn, regime flips), then CLOSES it before
    calling ``DuckDBLedgerRepository.load_forecast_component_gates`` /
    ``.load_forecast_residual_share`` — those open their own short-lived
    read-write connections (existing, unmodified interface), and DuckDB
    refuses a second connection to the same file with a different
    read_only configuration while the first is still open.

assumptions: Parallax 維持 local file-backed analytics，並以單一 owner process 執行 read-write 工作。
本次以 2026-07-14 擷取的 DuckDB 1.5.x current documentation 為版本邊界。
exclusions: 不在本次研究中導入或 benchmark Quack、DuckLake 或外部 catalog。
不把 D1 synthesis 或搜尋結果摘要當作 canonical evidence。

retrieval_occurrences.synthesis_excerpt:
# Concurrency Boundaries In A Single-Writer Local DuckDB Analytics Application

DuckDB is explicitly designed as an in-process analytical database, and its concurrency model reflects a strong bias toward single-process, multi-threaded execution with snapshot-isolated, optimistic multi-version concurrency control rather than the traditional multi-process client/server pattern.[29][29][20] For a local analytics application that assumes a single writer and potentially multiple readers, the critical boundary is not primarily at the level of transactions or isolation levels, but at the level of operating-system processes and file locks: the official documentation consistently states that a DuckDB database file supports either one read–write process or multiple read-only processes, but not a mixture of one writer and additional readers across processes.[2][41][43] Within a single process, however, DuckDB supports multiple concurrent writer threads that can perform appends, updates, schema changes, and bulk operations concurrently, coordinated by MVCC and optimistic concurrency control; appends on the same table are guaranteed never to conflict, while updates to the same rows can fail at commit time with transaction conflict errors rather than waiting on locks.[1][2][2][11] This report maps the documented process and concurrency boundary from official DuckDB sources, separates those sources from third‑party interpretation, and highlights hypotheses, possible contradictions, and concrete failure modes that a host application should re-verify with its own deterministic acceptance tests, without drawing a final engineering conclusion.

## DuckDB’s In-Process Design And High-Level Concurrency Model

### DuckDB As An In-Process OLAP Engine

DuckDB describes itself as “an in-process SQL OLAP database management system” that is “simple, feature-rich, fast & open source.”[29][29] The phrase “in-process” is central to understanding its concurrency boundary: unlike client–server systems such as PostgreSQL, DuckDB is designed to be embedded in the application process and to operate over local files, memory, and extensions as part of that process’s address space.[29][38] The official connection overview states that to use DuckDB, the application must first create a connection to a database, typically by calling the relevant client library (for example, `duckdb.connect` in Python) with either a path to a persistent database file or a special value (such as `:memory:`) for an in-memory database.[38][43] This connection object lives in the application process and orchestrates all transactional and concurrency control for that instance.

The in-process design implies that most concurrency is expected to be handled by threads within a single process rather than by multiple external clients connecting over network sockets.[29] DuckDB’s own performance tuning guidance emphasizes multi-threaded parallelism within a single connection and process, noting that “DuckDB will perform best when reusing the same database connection many times” and that disconnecting and reconnecting incurs overhead and destroys in-process caches.[8] This is a strong hint that DuckDB’s primary concurrency model is intra-process, thread-based and that frequent process-level connect/disconnect patterns are suboptimal.

The official concurrency documentation explicitly reflects this focus on single-process concurrency. It distinguishes between “multiple writer threads” and “multiple processes,” explaining that DuckDB supports “multiple writer threads using a combination of MVCC (Multi-Version Concurrency Control) and optimistic concurrency control,” but draws a different boundary for multi-process access, which will be discussed in detail later.[2][2][41] For a host application architect, this distinction is critical: DuckDB’s concurrency guarantees at the transactional level assume a single embedding process that may use many threads, while the process-level behavior is constraine

sources.title: DuckDB Quack remote protocol documentation source

summary.decision: Parallax 應維持單一 process 擁有原生 DuckDB file 的 read-write lifecycle；同一檔案的並行讀寫留在 owner process 內，以 transaction conflict 明確失敗並由 bounded retry 處理。外部 CLI／report consumer 不應假設可與 writer 同時直接開檔，應改讀 immutable export、snapshot 或 owner-mediated surface。Quack 提供未來的 multi-process client-server 路徑，但目前仍是 beta，不進 production boundary。
summary.human_recommendation: 把 single-writer 從設計慣例升級成 deterministic acceptance contract；現在不導入 Quack，待其穩定且能通過同一組 failure-path tests 再評估。
summary.human_status: Standard 循環確認核心 single-process boundary，並以現行官方文件修正 D1 過度絕對的 multi-process framing：Quack 已提供 multi-process write 路徑，但仍是 beta。

verification.disposition: D1 的 single-process hypothesis 觸發 Exa 精準搜尋；官方 concurrency 與 Quack 原始文件接受 C1、C3，並修正 C2 的絕對 framing：multi-process write 已有 Quack 路徑，但 beta 狀態不足以改變 Parallax 當前 production 決策。
